description:
Same mechanical application. Depends on fz-duq.2/.3. Combined into
one ticket because both are short and structurally identical.

## Replace `lower_cond` (`src/ir_lower.rs:1921`)

Two-shape pattern. Tail-position: each arm body lowered with
`is_tail=true`, finalize with Return. Non-tail: mint join fn,
finalize each arm with TailCall(join_fn, [v, ...captured]).

The cond's arm-test cascade (`arm_test_blocks` + Term::If between
them) stays as-is — that's intra-fn block flow with direct Gotos, no
CPS-split hazard. Only the arm BODIES (which can contain calls)
need the new join treatment.

## Replace `lower_with` (`src/ir_lower.rs:1975`)

Same shape for the main success body and the else-clause bodies —
both join via the new continuation-fn join.

`with_fail` block stays a block. It's reached only by direct Goto
from per-binding mismatch sites (no CPS-split between mismatch and
`with_fail`), so the block-level join is valid there. Only the
post-clause join needs the new shape.

## Test

A `cond`-with-call-in-arm-body test and a `with`-with-call-in-body
test, both today silently broken via Bug 2.

```fz
fn helper(), do: 7
fn route(n) do
  cond do
    n == 0 -> helper()
    true -> 99
  end
end
fn main() do
  print(route(0))
  print(route(5))
end
```

Expected: `7\n99\n`.

## Acceptance

- `cargo test --workspace` green.
- `cargo test --workspace golden_clif` zero drift.
- Both new fixtures pass interp + JIT.

## Removes

- `lower_cond`'s `join_b` allocation (`:1936-1937`) and Goto-to-join
  at `:1965`.
- `lower_with`'s `join_b` allocation (`:1988-1989`) and Goto-to-join
  sites at `:2035`, `:2071`.
- Duplicated per-arm env/terminated boilerplate now subsumed by the
  arm-finalizer helper.
title: fz-duq.4: lower_cond + lower_with use continuation-fn join
